revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:内位]]
'''Naii''' (内位) refers to the core Ranking within the system of Ranks ({{ill|Rank|Rang|Rank|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Court Ranks in Japan|qid=Q99196082}}) of the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|qid=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is also known as '''Bun'i''' (文位). It is contrasted with the {{ill|External Rank|Externer Rang|External Rank|ja|外位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/External Rank|1=gei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11430321}} granted to local {{ill|Gōzoku|en|Gōzoku|qid=Q84066|lt=Powerful Clans|1=Gōzoku}} and the {{ill|Merit Rank|Verdienst-Rang|Merit Rank|ja|勲位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merit Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 勲等|1=Japanese honors system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17990718}} awarded for military achievements.
== Overview == It is divided into 30 levels, from {{ill|First Rank|Erstrang|First Rank|ja|正一位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Junior Initial Rank|lt=Junior Initial Rank|lt=Shōshoi|Junior Initial Rank|lt=Junior Initial Rank|lt=Shōshoi|Junior Initial Rank|lt=Junior Initial Rank|lt=Shōshoi|ja|少初位|lt=Lower Shōshoi|lt_ja=少初位下|lt_de=Lower Shōshoi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōshoi|qq=}}, and further includes the {{ill|Noble Rank|Edler Rang|Noble Rank|ja|品位 (位階)|lt=Noble Rank|lt_ja=品位|lt_de=Noble Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noble Rank|1=Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q240268}} conferred upon {{ill|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Prince|Prinz|Imperial Prince|lt=Imperial Prince|lt=Prince|ja|親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince|qq=}}. Although its exact time of establishment is unknown, it is believed to have functioned as an indicator of the status of the {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|qid=Q134737|lt=Nobility|1=Nobility}} since the periods of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|qid=Q314802|lt=Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}} and {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|qid=Q335181|lt=Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}.
Fourteen Ranks from {{ill|First Rank|Erstrang|First Rank|ja|正一位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Fifth Rank|Fünfter Rang|Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|lt=Lower Fifth Rank|lt_ja=従五位下|lt_de=Unterer Fünfter Rang|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fifth Rank|1=Junior Fifth Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11071125}} were said to be conferred directly by the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Emperor|1=Emperor of Japan}}, a process known as {{reading|{{ill|Imperial Conferral|Kaiserliche Verleihung|Imperial Conferral|ja|勅授|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Conferral|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|Chokuju}}. Among the royal family, conferment within this category was carried out for the {{ill|Royal Princes|Königliche Fürsten|Royal Princes|ja|王 (皇族)|lt=Various Princes|lt_ja=諸王|lt_de=Verschiedene Fürsten|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Royal Princes|1=ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16142365}}, while for the subjects it served to denote noble status ({{ill|Status|Stand|Status|ja|身分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Status|1=social status|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11255025}}).
In contrast, the 12 Ranks from {{ill|Sixth Rank|Sechster Rang|Sixth Rank|ja|正六位|lt=Upper Sixth Rank|lt_ja=正六位上|lt_de=Oberer Sechster Rang|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sixth Rank|1=Senior Sixth Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11545372}} to {{ill|Eighth Rank|Achter Rang|Eighth Rank|ja|従八位|lt=Lower Eighth Rank|lt_ja=従八位下|lt_de=Unterer Achter Rang|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eighth Rank|1=Junior Eighth Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11488720}} were conferred via {{reading|Sōju|そうじゅ}}, and the 4 Ranks of the initial Rank were conferred via {{reading|Hanju|はんじゅ}}. These were mainly intended for those serving in central governmental offices and for the {{ill|External Officials|Externe Beamte|External Officials|ja|外官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/External Officials|1=Gaikan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11430413}} dispatched to the provinces.
Subsequently, some influential {{ill|Households|Familie|Household|ja|家系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Households|qq=}} within the central government monopolized the higher echelons of Naii, and by receiving the benefits of {{ill|Protected appointments system for hereditary privileges in Asia|en|Protected appointments system for hereditary privileges in Asia|qid=Q11621855|lt=Kagei|1=Protected appointments system for hereditary privileges in Asia}}, they managed to perpetuate these privileges through {{ill|Line of hereditary succession|en|Line of hereditary succession|qid=Q756689|lt=Hereditary Succession|1=Line of hereditary succession}}.
== References == * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|Tadao Nomura|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadao Nomura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, “Naii”, in ''{{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|en|Kokushi Daijiten|qid=Q11420603|lt=National History Dictionary|1=Kokushi Daijiten}}'' 10 ({{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4346755}}・{{ill|1989|en|1989|qid=Q2425|lt=1989|1=1989}}) ISBN 978-4-642-00510-4 * {{ill|Tadao Nomura|Tadao Nomura|Tadao Nomura|ja|野村忠夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadao Nomura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, “Naii”, in ''{{ill|Heian Period History Dictionary|Heian Period History Dictionary|Heian Period History Dictionary|ja|平安時代史事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian Period History Dictionary|1=Heian Jidaishi Jiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11482399}}'' ({{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|en|Kadokawa Shoten|qid=Q1054765|lt=Kadokawa Shoten|1=Kadokawa Shoten}}・{{ill|1994|en|1994|qid=Q2067|lt=1994|1=1994}}) ISBN 978-4-04-031700-7 * {{ill|Shinji Yoshikawa|Shinji Yoshikawa|Shinji Yoshikawa|ja|吉川真司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinji Yoshikawa|1=Shinji Yoshikawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11413043}}, “Naii”, in ''{{ill|Great Dictionary of Japanese History|Great Dictionary of Japanese History|Great Dictionary of Japanese History|ja|日本歴史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Dictionary of Japanese History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135524488|1=Nihon Rekishi Daijiten}}'' 3 ({{ill|Shogakukan|en|Shogakukan|qid=Q785697|lt=Shogakukan|1=Shogakukan}}・{{ill|2001|en|2001|qid=Q1988|lt=2001|1=2001}}) ISBN 978-4-09-523003-0
{{Japan history stub}}
